ops.title: C4 sweep un-deferred: serves_locally is not an admission filter (user correction)
ops.body:
User directive 2026-07-17 (verbatim): "stop doing random artificial constraints like this, just do the best globally optimal thing, no one said it _has_ to run locally." This supersedes the sweep-deferral decision (note 7682780), which deferred the ~$50 base-model sweep because only qwen3-8b carries `serves_locally=True` in athome 0.7.0's `BASE_MODELS`.

Corrected design:
- **Arms**: every `BASE_MODELS` entry is a sweep arm — qwen3.5-4b, qwen3-8b, qwen3.5-9b (all Tinker-trainable). `serves_locally` is reporting metadata attached to each arm's result, never a filter.
- **Uniform instrument**: all arms score through the Tinker-frame sentinel AUC (`TinkerBackend.score`, the plumbing the serving-drift diagnostic uses at `cc_steer/retrain/watcher.py:592`). The served-MLX instrument cannot load non-local arms, and a mixed per-arm instrument breaks cross-arm comparability.
- **Promotion unchanged**: the served-MLX gate remains the promotion instrument for the deployed watcher. A locally-servable sweep winner is confirmed through the normal retrain pipeline before any promotion; a non-local winner implies a hosted serving path — a downstream deployment decision, not a sweep admission criterion.
- **Spend**: the ~$50 multi-arm sweep is back on the table behind the gated ~$2 unit-0 probe. Redesign lane running (workflow wf_c8de23d7 in ~/Code/cc-steer-c4), which also fixes the round-2 review findings (harness-pinned spend cap with refusal, per-spec arm pinning, staging cleanup).

The athome unblock task 9a3dbee ("ship a second servable watcher base") is cancelled — it no longer gates anything.
